_meta.name: BobGuard Extended HIPAA Controls (Technical Safeguards)
_meta.extends: Adds bobguard.* fields for code-time enforcement and PR refusal
_meta.regulation_baseline: HIPAA Security Rule, 45 CFR Part 164 Subpart C (existing rule, 2013 revision)

controls.description: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain electronic protected health information to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights as specified in §164.308(a)(4) (Information Access Management).

code_patterns.comment: PHI route registered without auth middleware
Direct ORM access to PHI without RBAC check

bobguard.refusal_template: ❌ HIPAA §164.312(a)(1) — route {route} accesses ePHI without an access-control gate. Trust is built by saying no. Proposed fix: add `requireRole('clinician'|'admin')` middleware and an audit-log hook.
auditor_questions: How are access rights enforced at the application layer?
How is the principle of minimum necessary applied?
What is the workflow for granting and revoking access?

controls.description: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.

code_patterns.comment: Session created with service/shared account in PHI context

bobguard.refusal_template: ❌ HIPAA §164.312(a)(2)(i) — generic identifier '{value}' used in ePHI session creation. Each user must be uniquely identifiable. Proposed fix: route through identity provider and persist `subjectId`.
auditor_questions: How is each accessing user uniquely identified?
Are service accounts ever used in ePHI workflows? If so, how are they distinguished from human users in audit logs?

controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures for obtaining necessary electronic protected health information during an emergency.

bobguard.refusal_template: ⚠️ HIPAA §164.312(a)(2)(ii) — emergency access procedure marked as unimplemented at {file}:{line}. Proposed fix: implement break-glass with elevated audit logging.
auditor_questions: What is the emergency access procedure?
How are break-glass events flagged in the audit log?

controls.description: Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity.

bobguard.refusal_template: ⚠️ HIPAA §164.312(a)(2)(iii) — session at {file}:{line} lacks an inactivity-based logoff. Proposed fix: set `maxAge` ≤ 15 minutes for ePHI-handling sessions.
auditor_questions: What is the session inactivity timeout for users with ePHI access?

controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt electronic protected health information.

code_patterns.comment: Unencrypted PHI primitive type at rest
ORM write of PHI without encryption helper
PHI written to disk via plain filesystem call

bobguard.refusal_template: ❌ HIPAA §164.312(a)(2)(iv) — `{field}` is ePHI stored unencrypted at rest in {file}:{line}. Trust is built by saying no. Proposed fix: wrap with `encryptAtRest()` from `lib/phi-crypto.ts`. Forward-compat: under the 2024 HIPAA Security Rule NPRM this control is proposed to move from addressable to required; BobGuard already enforces the stricter standard.
auditor_questions: How is ePHI encrypted at rest?
What is the key management mechanism (KMS, HSM, app-managed)?
How is encryption key rotation evidenced?

controls.description: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

code_patterns.comment: PHI route with auth but no audit-log hook

bobguard.refusal_template: ❌ HIPAA §164.312(b) — PHI route at {file}:{line} does not emit an audit event. Trust is built by saying no. Proposed fix: invoke `audit.log({ actor, action, resource, outcome })` before returning.
auditor_questions: What activity is logged for ePHI access, modification, and deletion?
What is the log retention period?
How are audit logs themselves protected from tampering?

controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to protect electronic protected health information from improper alteration or destruction.

code_patterns.comment: Hard delete of records — review for ePHI

bobguard.refusal_template: ⚠️ HIPAA §164.312(c)(1) — hard-delete on {file}:{line} risks ePHI integrity. Proposed fix: switch to soft-delete with tombstone + integrity hash.
auditor_questions: How are ePHI records protected from improper alteration or destruction?

controls.control_name: Mechanism to Authenticate Electronic Protected Health Information (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement electronic mechanisms to corroborate that electronic protected health information has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner.

bobguard.refusal_template: ⚠️ HIPAA §164.312(c)(2) — integrity verification not detected for ePHI store at {file}. Proposed fix: store SHA-256 hash alongside record; verify on read.
auditor_questions: How is the integrity of ePHI verified at rest?

controls.description: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed.

bobguard.refusal_template: ❌ HIPAA §164.312(d) — authentication at {file}:{line} is single-factor for an ePHI-bearing system. Trust is built by saying no. Proposed fix: gate with TOTP/WebAuthn MFA. Forward-compat: the 2024 NPRM proposes explicit MFA requirement; BobGuard enforces it now.
auditor_questions: What authentication factors are used for ePHI access?
Is MFA required for all users with ePHI access?

controls.description: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to electronic protected health information that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.

code_patterns.comment: Outbound URL — verify HTTPS and not bare HTTP
Webhook endpoint without TLS enforcement check

bobguard.refusal_template: ❌ HIPAA §164.312(e)(1) — ePHI transmitted over plain HTTP at {file}:{line}. Trust is built by saying no. Proposed fix: enforce HTTPS/TLS 1.2+ on this transport.
auditor_questions: How is ePHI protected during transmission?

controls.description: Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted electronic protected health information is not improperly modified without detection until disposed of.

bobguard.refusal_template: ⚠️ HIPAA §164.312(e)(2)(i) — outbound message at {file} lacks a message authentication code. Proposed fix: sign payload with HMAC-SHA256.
auditor_questions: How is the integrity of ePHI verified during transmission?

controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt electronic protected health information whenever deemed appropriate.

bobguard.refusal_template: ❌ HIPAA §164.312(e)(2)(ii) — ePHI in transit uses TLS < 1.2 or no TLS at {file}:{line}. Trust is built by saying no. Proposed fix: enforce `minVersion: 'TLSv1.2'` (TLS 1.3 preferred).
auditor_questions: What TLS configuration protects ePHI in transit?
